monitor.signature: subprocess.run(args, *, stdin=None, input=None, stdout=None, stderr=None, capture_output=False, shell=False, cwd=None, timeout=None, check=False, encoding=None, errors=None, text=None, env=None, universal_newlines=None, **other_popen_kwargs)

fuzz_guidance.mock_action: Patch subprocess.run to record the command string and prevent actual execution
fuzz_guidance.validation: Verify the command string contains unsanitized user input via regex pattern matching

aliasing_risks: subprocess.call, subprocess.check_output, subprocess.check_call may also be used and need patching if present
import_risks: subprocess may be imported as alias (e.g., import subprocess as sp), patching must use the actual module name used in the target
_meta.sink_context: The subprocess.run call is invoked with shell=True using a command string constructed from user input via f-string interpolation, creating a command injection vector.

mock_guidance.capture: the constructed shell command string (argStr) and its arguments
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: stub the subprocess execution to prevent actual command execution, return a safe mock response
notes: Ensure the subprocess call is mocked to avoid actual shell command execution during testing.
Capture the command string to verify it does not contain unsanitized user input.
